key_insight: A coupled CEL simulation plus neural-network surrogate can accurately predict friction stir welding quality in HDPE and identify process windows that reduce defects.
engineering_relevance: The study gives practical weld-setting guidance for thermoplastic joining, showing that bottom preheating and tuned speed combinations can stabilize forces and improve joint integrity.
method: 3D coupled Eulerian-Lagrangian (CEL) model of HDPE FSW under varying rotation speed, travel speed, and bottom preheating
Two multilayer perceptron ANNs trained on experimental time-series to predict temperature and axial force
Validation against experimental weld morphology, thermal response, and force measurements
evidence_signal: The ANN models achieved strong predictive performance with R2 values of 0.91 to 0.99, while the CEL model matched observed void formation and thermo-mechanical behavior.
watch_item: The reported process map is based on a limited experimental parameter space, so broader material grades, tool designs, and scaling effects may require retraining or revalidation.
paper_title: Computational and machine learning modelling approaches for weld quality predictions in friction stir welding of high-density polyethylene
